assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT true of Bernini's *Ecstasy of Saint Teresa*?**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9d421f3f190802deb6234b3225833fe.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Bernini creates different illusionistic surface textures to contrast the divine presence of the angel and St. Teresa's materiality.  **

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/bernini-ecstasy-of-st-theresa)**

choices.content: He combines elements of architecture, sculpture, and painting in situating the sculpture in the chapel with polychromed marble, fresco, and stained glass.
The subject is of a saint, canonized in 1622, having a vision of an angel.  Bernini based his sculpture on St. Teresa's written accounts of the vision.
Both the text and Bernini's sculpture use the physical body and sexual symbolism to convey the spiritual experience.
All of the marble was finished in the same way, unifying the figures with the architecture in the chapel.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Bernini expressed ideas of [[☃ dropdown 1]] by using the ornate elements of the church to express a direct relationship between the individual and the spiritual realm.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9d421f3f190802deb6234b3225833fe.png)
hints:
**Bernini expressed ideas of the Counter-Reformation by using the ornate elements of the church to express a direct relationship between the individual and the spiritual realm.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/bernini-ecstasy-of-st-theresa)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Bernini involved the viewer through the entire space of the chapel which suggests we are part of the audience witnessing the spiritual event.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/f9d421f3f190802deb6234b3225833fe.png)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Bernini created a complete experience with this sculpture, which allowed viewers to feel like they were witnessing the vision themselves.**

**[Watch this video to review](/humanities/monarchy-enlightenment/baroque-art1/baroque-italy/v/bernini-ecstasy-of-st-theresa)**
